content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. The only difference between this and the easy version is that here, you're given only N−1N-1N−1 elements of AAA, and have to solve the problem for each value of the last element.**

Consider a tree on NNN vertices, with vertex iii having the value AiA_iAi​ associated with it.

The score of the tree with respect to array AAA, denoted f(A)f(A)f(A), is defined as follows:


For each edge (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) of the tree, give it the value Au+AvA_u + A_vAu​+Av​.
The score of the tree is then the greatest common divisor (GCD) of the values of all the edges.
You are given a tree on NNN vertices, an array AAA of length N−1N-1N−1, and an integer MMM.

It is guaranteed that max⁡(A)≤M\max(A) \leq Mmax(A)≤M.

For each x=1,2,…,Mx = 1, 2, \ldots, Mx=1,2,…,M, solve the following problem independently:


Append xxx to AAA, forming an array of length NNN where AN=xA_N = xAN​=x.

Compute the minimum value of f(A)f(A)f(A) across all rearrangements of this array AAA.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains N−1N-1N−1 space-separated integers A1,A2,…,AN−1A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{N-1}A1​,A2​,…,AN−1​.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the tree. The iii-th of them contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between these two vertices.




Output Format
For each test case, output MMM space-separated integers on a new line: the iii-th of them should represent the minimum possible value of f(A)f(A)f(A) across all rearrangements of AAA, if AN=iA_N = iAN​=i.
constraints: The input edges describe a tree on the vertex set {1,2,…,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,…,N}.
The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM across all tests both won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.
